steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: You tell Mrs. Henderson the truth. The landlord played a trick on Jake. They did the same thing to Ryan. They pushed him out.
steps.text_cn: 你把真相告诉Henderson太太。房东对Jake耍了手段，他们当年也对Ryan做过同样的事，把他逼走了。

steps.instruction: "They did the same thing to Ryan. They pushed him out." — What happened to Mrs. Henderson's son?
steps.feedback: Right! 'Pushed out' means they forced her son to leave. / 对！'Pushed out' 意思是他们逼她儿子离开了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Push out' means to force someone to leave — Ryan had to go. / 'Push out' 意思是逼某人离开——Ryan不得不走。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: No one helped Ryan back then...

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: Maybe you should tell him. Things are different now.
steps.text_cn: 也许你应该告诉他。现在不一样了。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: He won't answer my calls. We argued when he left.
steps.text_cn: 他不会接我的电话。他离开的时候我们吵了一架。

steps.instruction: Mrs. Henderson says her son won't answer. She looks sad. What do you offer?
steps.feedback: Brave and kind! You offered to help a mother call her son. / 又勇敢又善良！你提议帮一位母亲打电话给儿子。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! She needs help calling her son — 'I can call with you' is the best support. / 她需要帮助打电话给儿子——'I can call with you' 是最好的支持。

options.label: B
options.text: I can wash up for you.

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: I can call with you. Do you want to try?
steps.text_cn: 我可以陪你一起打。你想试试吗？

steps.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
steps.speech_text: Match these words: knocks on your door, come round, come in.
steps.feedback: Great! You know the difference between knocking, visiting, and entering. / 很好！你知道敲门、来访和进来的区别了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Knocks on your door' = 敲你的门, 'come round' = 来访, 'come in' = 进来。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: He's coming to visit. Next month.

steps.instruction: "He's coming to visit next month." — What will Ryan do?
steps.feedback: Yes! Ryan will visit his mum — the family will be together again. / 对！Ryan要来看妈妈——一家人又能团聚了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Coming to visit' means Ryan will come to see his mother. / 'Coming to visit' 意思是Ryan会来看望他的母亲。
steps.review_word: He will come to see his mother

options.label: A
options.text: He will come to see his mother

options.label: B
options.text: He will move to a new flat

options.label: C
options.text: He will close his door forever

steps.instruction: You helped Mrs. Henderson call her son. In the US, what does this show about neighbors?
steps.feedback: Yes! In the US, neighbors often become your support system — like a second family. / 对！在美国，邻居常常成为你的支持系统——像第二个家。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! In the US, neighbors can be very close — helping each other is what makes a place feel like home. / 在美国，邻居可以非常亲近——互相帮助是让一个地方有家的感觉的原因。
steps.review_word: Good neighbors can become like family

options.label: A
options.text: Good neighbors can become like family

options.label: C
options.text: Only family can help with problems

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: Jake hears the news. "Wow. You are only here for one month. But you fixed a big problem. We couldn't do that for two years." You say: "I just dialed the phone." Jake: "Yeah. But it worked."
steps.text_cn: Jake听到这个消息后说："哇。你才来一个月，却解决了一个大问题。我们两年都没做到。" 你说："我只是拨了个电话。" Jake说："是啊，但它有用。"

steps.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
steps.answer: They pushed him out of his apartment.
steps.normalized_answer: they pushed him out of his apartment
steps.feedback: Perfect! 'Push out' = force to leave. 'Pull' is the opposite. / 完美！'Push out' = 逼迫离开。'Pull' 是相反的动作。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Pushed him out' is correct — they forced Ryan to leave. 'Pulled' means the opposite direction. / 'Pushed him out' 才对——他们逼Ryan离开了。'Pulled' 是相反方向。
steps.review_word: They pushed him out of his apartment.

steps.instruction: 请跟读：I can call with you.（重音：call / you；语速：慢速，语气温暖、给人安心感）
steps.feedback: Warm and steady! You sound supportive. / 温暖又稳定！你听起来很有支持感。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Try again: I can CALL with YOU. Stress 'call' and 'you'. / 再试一次：重读CALL和YOU。
